mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high retail auto sales and low federal funds rates indicate strong consumer credit expansion and durable goods demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in > 60% of cases when RSAFS is in the top quartile and FEDFUNDS is in the bottom quartile.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate output, which predict positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during periods where annual GDP growth falls below 1.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal renewed construction activity and upstream supply chain demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in > 55% of months where HOUST increases by more than 5 percent sequentially.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in > 60% of high-inflation months without subsequent rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and real GDP confirms broad-based economic expansion and rising corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in > 50% of quarters when both series grow simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production coinciding with rising unemployment indicates a contracting output gap and weakening corporate revenues, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during downturn phases.
failure_condition: Short-term equity returns remain positive in > 55% of instances when both indicators deteriorate simultaneously.
